search_text: Think-Aloud Verbalizations for Identifying User Experience Problems: Effects of Language Proficiency with Chinese Non-Native English Speakers 用于识别用户体验问题的有声思考言语表达：语言能力对中国非英语母语者的影响 Subtle patterns in users' think-aloud (TA) verbalizations (i.e., utterances) are shown to be telltale signs of user experience (UX) problems and used to build artificial intelligence (AI) models or AI-assisted tools to help UX evaluators identify UX problems automatically or semi-automatically. Despite the potential of such verbalization patterns, they were uncovered with native English speakers. As most people who speak English are non-native speakers, it is important to investigate whether similar patterns exist in non-native English speakers' TA verbalizations. As a first step to answer this question, we conducted think-aloud usability testing with Chinese non-native English speakers and native English speakers using three common TA protocols. We compared their verbalizations and UX problems that they encountered to understand the effects of language and TA protocols. Our findings show that both language groups had similar amounts and proportions of verbalization categories, encountered similar problems, and had similar verbalization patterns that indicate UX problems. Furthermore, TA protocols did not significantly affect the correlations between verbalizations and problems. Based on the findings, we present three design implications for UX practitioners and the design of AI-assisted analysis tools. 用户有声思考 (TA) 言语（即话语）中的微妙模式被证明是用户体验 (UX) 问题的明显迹象，并用于构建人工智能 (AI) 模型或人工智能辅助工具，以帮助 UX 评估人员自动或半自动识别 UX 问题。尽管这种语言模式具有潜力，但以英语为母语的人却发现了它们。由于大多数说英语的人都是非母语人士，因此研究非英语母语人士的助教言语中是否存在类似的模式非常重要。作为回答这个问题的第一步，我们使用三种常见的 TA 协议对中国非英语母语者和英语母语者进行了有声思考可用性测试。我们比较了他们的言语表达和遇到的用户体验问题，以了解语言和 TA 协议的影响。我们的研究结果表明，两个语言组的言语类别数量和比例相似，遇到相似的问题，并且具有相似的言语模式（表明用户体验问题）。此外，TA 协议并没有显着影响言语和问题之间的相关性。根据研究结果，我们为用户体验从业者和人工智能辅助分析工具的设计提出了三种设计含义。 研究中文非英语母语者在思考出声（TA）中的口头表达模式是否能有效识别用户体验问题，并与英语母语者进行对比分析。 Mingming Fan,Lingyun Zhu cs.HC

title: Learning from Imperfect Demonstrations via Adversarial Confidence Transfer
title_zh: 通过对抗性置信度转移从不完美的演示中学习
abstract: Existing learning from demonstration algorithms usually assume access to expert demonstrations. However, this assumption is limiting in many real-world applications since the collected demonstrations may be suboptimal or even consist of failure cases. We therefore study the problem of learning from imperfect demonstrations by learning a confidence predictor. Specifically, we rely on demonstrations along with their confidence values from a different correspondent environment (source environment) to learn a confidence predictor for the environment we aim to learn a policy in (target environment -- where we only have unlabeled demonstrations.) We learn a common latent space through adversarial distribution matching of multi-length partial trajectories to enable the transfer of confidence across source and target environments. The learned confidence reweights the demonstrations to enable learning more from informative demonstrations and discarding the irrelevant ones. Our experiments in three simulated environments and a real robot reaching task demonstrate that our approach learns a policy with the highest expected return.
summary: Existing learning from demonstration algorithms usually assume access to expert demonstrations. However, this assumption is limiting in many real-world applications since the collected demonstrations may be suboptimal or even consist of failure cases. We therefore study the problem of learning from imperfect demonstrations by learning a confidence predictor. Specifically, we rely on demonstrations along with their confidence values from a different correspondent environment (source environment) to learn a confidence predictor for the environment we aim to learn a policy in (target environment -- where we only have unlabeled demonstrations.) We learn a common latent space through adversarial distribution matching of multi-length partial trajectories to enable the transfer of confidence across source and target environments. The learned confidence reweights the demonstrations to enable learning more from informative demonstrations and discarding the irrelevant ones. Our experiments in three simulated environments and a real robot reaching task demonstrate that our approach learns a policy with the highest expected return.
abstract_zh: 现有的演示算法学习通常假设可以访问专家演示。然而，这种假设在许多实际应用中受到限制，因为收集的演示可能不是最优的，甚至包含失败案例。因此，我们通过学习置信度预测器来研究从不完美的演示中学习的问题。具体来说，我们依靠来自不同对应环境（源环境）的演示及其置信值来学习我们旨在学习策略的环境（目标环境 - 我们只有未标记的演示）的置信预测器。我们通过多长度部分轨迹的对抗性分布匹配来学习公共潜在空间，以实现源环境和目标环境之间的置信度转移。学到的置信度会重新衡量演示，以便能够从信息丰富的演示中学到更多知识，并丢弃不相关的演示。我们在三个模拟环境和真实机器人到达任务中的实验表明，我们的方法学习了具有最高预期回报的策略。

AI.tldr: 提出一种通过对抗置信度迁移学习从不完美演示中学习策略的方法。
AI.motivation: 现有算法依赖专家演示，但实际应用中演示可能次优或包含失败案例，需学习如何处理不完美演示。
AI.method: 利用源环境演示及置信度，通过对抗分布匹配学习共同潜在空间，实现跨环境置信度迁移，并用置信度重新加权演示。
AI.result: 实验显示方法在模拟环境和真实机器人任务中取得最高预期回报。
AI.conclusion: 该方法能有效从不完美演示中学习，提升策略性能。

authors_text: Jinghao Wen,Dongning Ma,Sizhe Zhang,Hasshi Sudler,Xun Jiao
title: Proof-of-Useful-Work Blockchain for Trustworthy Biomedical Hyperdimensional Computing
title_zh: 用于可信赖的生物医学超维计算的有用工作量证明区块链
abstract: Hyperdimensional Computing (HDC) is a promising bio-inspired learning paradigm for its advantage of balancing performance and efficiency and has been increasingly applied to the bio-medical domain. In bio-medical applications, trustworthiness such as replicability and verifiability of the trained learning models is crucial. In this work, we introduce HDCoin, the first proof-of-useful-work blockchain framework for HDC. With HDCoin, we transform the conventional energy-wasteful mining process into a competitive process for developing high accuracy, trustworthy and verifiable hyperdimensional models. We explore four diverse biomedical datasets, and conduct an extensive design-space exploration of key HDC hyperparameters of blockchain miners such as dimensionality, learning rate, and retraining iterations for model performance, adaptive mining difficulty and fairness on proof-of-useful-work.
summary: Hyperdimensional Computing (HDC) is a promising bio-inspired learning paradigm for its advantage of balancing performance and efficiency and has been increasingly applied to the bio-medical domain. In bio-medical applications, trustworthiness such as replicability and verifiability of the trained learning models is crucial. In this work, we introduce HDCoin, the first proof-of-useful-work blockchain framework for HDC. With HDCoin, we transform the conventional energy-wasteful mining process into a competitive process for developing high accuracy, trustworthy and verifiable hyperdimensional models. We explore four diverse biomedical datasets, and conduct an extensive design-space exploration of key HDC hyperparameters of blockchain miners such as dimensionality, learning rate, and retraining iterations for model performance, adaptive mining difficulty and fairness on proof-of-useful-work.
abstract_zh: 超维计算（HDC）是一种很有前景的仿生学习范式，因其平衡性能和效率的优势，已越来越多地应用于生物医学领域。在生物医学应用中，经过训练的学习模型的可复制性和可验证性等可信度至关重要。在这项工作中，我们介绍 HDCoin，这是 HDC 的第一个有用工作量证明区块链框架。通过 HDCoin，我们将传统的能源浪费采矿过程转变为开发高精度、值得信赖和可验证的超维模型的竞争过程。我们探索了四个不同的生物医学数据集，并对区块链矿工的关键 HDC 超参数进行了广泛的设计空间探索，例如维度、学习率和模型性能的再训练迭代、自适应挖掘难度和有用工作证明的公平性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出HDCoin区块链框架，将传统区块链的能源密集型挖矿过程转化为开发高精度、可验证的超维计算模型的竞争过程，以提升生物医学领域的计算可信度。
AI.motivation: 生物医学应用中训练模型的可重复性和可验证性至关重要，而传统区块链挖矿过程能源消耗大且缺乏实际价值产出，需寻找更高效的信任机制。
AI.method: 设计HDCoin框架，通过调整超维计算的维度、学习率、重训练次数等关键参数，结合区块链技术实现模型性能优化与公平的挖矿难度自适应机制。
AI.result: 在四个生物医学数据集上验证了HDCoin的有效性，证明其能提升模型准确性与可信度，同时实现挖矿过程的能源效率与公平性平衡。
AI.conclusion: HDCoin为生物医学超维计算提供了可持续、可信的区块链解决方案，通过将挖矿转化为实际模型训练，解决了传统方法的能源浪费与信任问题。

result: 在四个生物医学数据集上验证了HDCoin的有效性，证明其能提升模型准确性与可信度，同时实现挖矿过程的能源效率与公平性平衡。
conclusion: HDCoin为生物医学超维计算提供了可持续、可信的区块链解决方案，通过将挖矿转化为实际模型训练，解决了传统方法的能源浪费与信任问题。

title: A comprehensive survey on computational learning methods for analysis of gene expression data
title_zh: 用于分析基因表达数据的计算学习方法的综合综述
abstract: Computational analysis methods including machine learning have a significant impact in the fields of genomics and medicine. High-throughput gene expression analysis methods such as microarray technology and RNA sequencing produce enormous amounts of data. Traditionally, statistical methods are used for comparative analysis of gene expression data. However, more complex analysis for classification of sample observations, or discovery of feature genes requires sophisticated computational approaches. In this review, we compile various statistical and computational tools used in analysis of expression microarray data. Even though the methods are discussed in the context of expression microarrays, they can also be applied for the analysis of RNA sequencing and quantitative proteomics datasets. We discuss the types of missing values, and the methods and approaches usually employed in their imputation. We also discuss methods of data normalization, feature selection, and feature extraction. Lastly, methods of classification and class discovery along with their evaluation parameters are described in detail. We believe that this detailed review will help the users to select appropriate methods for preprocessing and analysis of their data based on the expected outcome.
summary: Computational analysis methods including machine learning have a significant impact in the fields of genomics and medicine. High-throughput gene expression analysis methods such as microarray technology and RNA sequencing produce enormous amounts of data. Traditionally, statistical methods are used for comparative analysis of gene expression data. However, more complex analysis for classification of sample observations, or discovery of feature genes requires sophisticated computational approaches. In this review, we compile various statistical and computational tools used in analysis of expression microarray data. Even though the methods are discussed in the context of expression microarrays, they can also be applied for the analysis of RNA sequencing and quantitative proteomics datasets. We discuss the types of missing values, and the methods and approaches usually employed in their imputation. We also discuss methods of data normalization, feature selection, and feature extraction. Lastly, methods of classification and class discovery along with their evaluation parameters are described in detail. We believe that this detailed review will help the users to select appropriate methods for preprocessing and analysis of their data based on the expected outcome.
abstract_zh: 包括机器学习在内的计算分析方法对基因组学和医学领域具有重大影响。微阵列技术和RNA测序等高通量基因表达分析方法会产生大量数据。传统上，统计方法用于基因表达数据的比较分析。然而，对样本观察进行分类或发现特征基因的更复杂的分析需要复杂的计算方法。在这篇综述中，我们编译了用于分析表达微阵列数据的各种统计和计算工具。尽管这些方法是在表达微阵列的背景下讨论的，但它们也可以应用于 RNA 测序和定量蛋白质组数据集的分析。我们讨论缺失值的类型以及通常用于插补的方法和途径。我们还讨论了数据标准化、特征选择和特征提取的方法。最后，详细描述了分类和类发现的方法及其评估参数。我们相信，这种详细的审查将帮助用户根据预期结果选择适当的方法来预处理和分析数据。

AI.tldr: 本文系统综述了计算学习方法在基因表达数据分析中的应用，涵盖数据预处理、特征选择、分类等方法，并讨论其适用性。
AI.motivation: 传统统计方法难以满足复杂分析需求（如样本分类和特征基因发现），且现有工具分散，需系统性总结以指导用户选择合适方法。
AI.method: 收集并整理微阵列数据相关统计与计算工具，讨论缺失值填补、数据归一化、特征选择/提取、分类及类别发现方法，并详细描述评估参数。

search_text: A comprehensive survey on computational learning methods for analysis of gene expression data 用于分析基因表达数据的计算学习方法的综合综述 Computational analysis methods including machine learning have a significant impact in the fields of genomics and medicine. High-throughput gene expression analysis methods such as microarray technology and RNA sequencing produce enormous amounts of data. Traditionally, statistical methods are used for comparative analysis of gene expression data. However, more complex analysis for classification of sample observations, or discovery of feature genes requires sophisticated computational approaches. In this review, we compile various statistical and computational tools used in analysis of expression microarray data. Even though the methods are discussed in the context of expression microarrays, they can also be applied for the analysis of RNA sequencing and quantitative proteomics datasets. We discuss the types of missing values, and the methods and approaches usually employed in their imputation. We also discuss methods of data normalization, feature selection, and feature extraction. Lastly, methods of classification and class discovery along with their evaluation parameters are described in detail. We believe that this detailed review will help the users to select appropriate methods for preprocessing and analysis of their data based on the expected outcome. 包括机器学习在内的计算分析方法对基因组学和医学领域具有重大影响。微阵列技术和RNA测序等高通量基因表达分析方法会产生大量数据。传统上，统计方法用于基因表达数据的比较分析。然而，对样本观察进行分类或发现特征基因的更复杂的分析需要复杂的计算方法。在这篇综述中，我们编译了用于分析表达微阵列数据的各种统计和计算工具。尽管这些方法是在表达微阵列的背景下讨论的，但它们也可以应用于 RNA 测序和定量蛋白质组数据集的分析。我们讨论缺失值的类型以及通常用于插补的方法和途径。我们还讨论了数据标准化、特征选择和特征提取的方法。最后，详细描述了分类和类发现的方法及其评估参数。我们相信，这种详细的审查将帮助用户根据预期结果选择适当的方法来预处理和分析数据。 本文系统综述了计算学习方法在基因表达数据分析中的应用，涵盖数据预处理、特征选择、分类等方法，并讨论其适用性。 Nikita Bhandari,Rahee Walambe,Ketan Kotecha,Satyajeet Khare q-bio.GN,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Sean I. Young,Adrian V. Dalca,Enzo Ferrante,Polina Golland,Christopher A. Metzler,Bruce Fischl,Juan Eugenio Iglesias
title: Supervision by Denoising for Medical Image Segmentation
title_zh: 医学图像分割的去噪监督
comment: To appear in the IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
comments: To appear in the IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Learning-based image reconstruction models, such as those based on the U-Net, require a large set of labeled images if good generalization is to be guaranteed. In some imaging domains, however, labeled data with pixel- or voxel-level label accuracy are scarce due to the cost of acquiring them. This problem is exacerbated further in domains like medical imaging, where there is no single ground truth label, resulting in large amounts of repeat variability in the labels. Therefore, training reconstruction networks to generalize better by learning from both labeled and unlabeled examples (called semi-supervised learning) is problem of practical and theoretical interest. However, traditional semi-supervised learning methods for image reconstruction often necessitate handcrafting a differentiable regularizer specific to some given imaging problem, which can be extremely time-consuming. In this work, we propose "supervision by denoising" (SUD), a framework that enables us to supervise reconstruction models using their own denoised output as soft labels. SUD unifies stochastic averaging and spatial denoising techniques under a spatio-temporal denoising framework and alternates denoising and model weight update steps in an optimization framework for semi-supervision. As example applications, we apply SUD to two problems arising from biomedical imaging -- anatomical brain reconstruction (3D) and cortical parcellation (2D) -- to demonstrate a significant improvement in the image reconstructions over supervised-only and stochastic averaging baselines.
summary: Learning-based image reconstruction models, such as those based on the U-Net, require a large set of labeled images if good generalization is to be guaranteed. In some imaging domains, however, labeled data with pixel- or voxel-level label accuracy are scarce due to the cost of acquiring them. This problem is exacerbated further in domains like medical imaging, where there is no single ground truth label, resulting in large amounts of repeat variability in the labels. Therefore, training reconstruction networks to generalize better by learning from both labeled and unlabeled examples (called semi-supervised learning) is problem of practical and theoretical interest. However, traditional semi-supervised learning methods for image reconstruction often necessitate handcrafting a differentiable regularizer specific to some given imaging problem, which can be extremely time-consuming. In this work, we propose "supervision by denoising" (SUD), a framework that enables us to supervise reconstruction models using their own denoised output as soft labels. SUD unifies stochastic averaging and spatial denoising techniques under a spatio-temporal denoising framework and alternates denoising and model weight update steps in an optimization framework for semi-supervision. As example applications, we apply SUD to two problems arising from biomedical imaging -- anatomical brain reconstruction (3D) and cortical parcellation (2D) -- to demonstrate a significant improvement in the image reconstructions over supervised-only and stochastic averaging baselines.
abstract_zh: 基于学习的图像重建模型，例如基于 U-Net 的模型，如果要保证良好的泛化能力，就需要大量标记图像。然而，在某些成像领域，由于获取成本较高，具有像素或体素级标签精度的标签数据很少。这个问题在医学成像等领域进一步加剧，因为这些领域没有单一的真实标签，导致标签中存在大量的重复变异。因此，通过从标记和未标记示例中学习（称为半监督学习）来训练重建网络以更好地泛化是具有实践和理论意义的问题。然而，用于图像重建的传统半监督学习方法通​​常需要针对某些给定的成像问题手工制作可微的正则化器，这可能非常耗时。在这项工作中，我们提出了“去噪监督”（SUD），这是一个框架，使我们能够使用其自己的去噪输出作为软标签来监督重建模型。 SUD 将随机平均和空间去噪技术统一在时空去噪框架下，并在半监督优化框架中交替去噪和模型权重更新步骤。作为示例应用，我们将 SUD 应用于生物医学成像产生的两个问题——大脑解剖重建 (3D) 和皮质分割 (2D)——以证明图像重建相对于仅监督和随机平均基线的显着改进。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为SUD的去噪监督框架，用于解决医学图像分割中标签数据不足的问题。
AI.motivation: 医学图像领域缺乏精确的像素级标签数据，且传统半监督方法需要复杂的手工设计正则化器，限制了模型泛化能力。
AI.method: 通过将模型自身生成的去噪结果作为软标签，结合时空去噪框架与交替优化机制，实现对重建模型的半监督训练。
AI.result: 在脑部解剖重建（3D）和皮层分区（2D）任务中，SUD显著优于纯监督和随机平均基线方法。
AI.conclusion: SUD框架有效利用了未标记数据，为医学图像分割提供了高效且通用的半监督学习解决方案。

title: Deep Deterministic Independent Component Analysis for Hyperspectral Unmixing
title_zh: 高光谱分解的深度确定性独立分量分析
abstract: We develop a new neural network based independent component analysis (ICA) method by directly minimizing the dependence amongst all extracted components. Using the matrix-based R{é}nyi's $α$-order entropy functional, our network can be directly optimized by stochastic gradient descent (SGD), without any variational approximation or adversarial training. As a solid application, we evaluate our ICA in the problem of hyperspectral unmixing (HU) and refute a statement that "\emph{ICA does not play a role in unmixing hyperspectral data}", which was initially suggested by \cite{nascimento2005does}. Code and additional remarks of our DDICA is available at https://github.com/hongmingli1995/DDICA.
summary: We develop a new neural network based independent component analysis (ICA) method by directly minimizing the dependence amongst all extracted components. Using the matrix-based R{é}nyi's $α$-order entropy functional, our network can be directly optimized by stochastic gradient descent (SGD), without any variational approximation or adversarial training. As a solid application, we evaluate our ICA in the problem of hyperspectral unmixing (HU) and refute a statement that "\emph{ICA does not play a role in unmixing hyperspectral data}", which was initially suggested by \cite{nascimento2005does}. Code and additional remarks of our DDICA is available at https://github.com/hongmingli1995/DDICA.
abstract_zh: 我们开发了一种新的基于神经网络的独立成分分析（ICA）方法，通过直接最小化所有提取成分之间的依赖性。使用基于矩阵的 R{é}nyi 的 $α$ 阶熵函数，我们的网络可以通过随机梯度下降（SGD）直接优化，无需任何变分逼近或对抗性训练。作为一个可靠的应用，我们在高光谱分解（HU）问题中评估了我们的 ICA，并反驳了“\emph{ICA 在分解高光谱数据中不起作用}”的说法，该说法最初由 \cite{nascimento2005does} 提出。我们的 DDICA 的代码和附加说明可在 https://github.com/hongmingli1995/DDICA 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度神经网络的独立成分分析（ICA）方法，用于高光谱解混任务，并验证ICA在该领域的有效性。
AI.motivation: 反驳传统观点'ICA无法用于高光谱解混'，并改进现有ICA方法以更高效处理高光谱数据的解混问题。
AI.method: 设计基于矩阵形式Rényi熵的深度确定性ICA框架，通过直接最小化成分间依赖性，利用SGD优化而无需对抗训练或变分近似。
AI.result: 在高光谱解混实验中验证方法有效性，证明ICA可作为解混工具，并开源代码供复现。
AI.conclusion: DDICA方法证实了ICA在高光谱解混中的可行性，为后续研究提供新的优化方向和实现范式。

Using the matrix-based R{é}nyi's $α$-order entropy functional, our network can be directly optimized by stochastic gradient descent (SGD), without any variational approximation or adversarial training. As a solid application, we evaluate our ICA in the problem of hyperspectral unmixing (HU) and refute a statement that "\emph{ICA does not play a role in unmixing hyperspectral data}", which was initially suggested by \cite{nascimento2005does}. Code and additional remarks of our DDICA is available at https://github.com/hongmingli1995/DDICA. 我们开发了一种新的基于神经网络的独立成分分析（ICA）方法，通过直接最小化所有提取成分之间的依赖性。使用基于矩阵的 R{é}nyi 的 $α$ 阶熵函数，我们的网络可以通过随机梯度下降（SGD）直接优化，无需任何变分逼近或对抗性训练。作为一个可靠的应用，我们在高光谱分解（HU）问题中评估了我们的 ICA，并反驳了“\emph{ICA 在分解高光谱数据中不起作用}”的说法，该说法最初由 \cite{nascimento2005does} 提出。我们的 DDICA 的代码和附加说明可在 https://github.com/hongmingli1995/DDICA 获取。 本文提出一种基于深度神经网络的独立成分分析（ICA）方法，用于高光谱解混任务，并验证ICA在该领域的有效性。 Hongming Li,Shujian Yu,Jose C. Principe eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: Mitchell L. Gordon,Michelle S. Lam,Joon Sung Park,Kayur Patel,Jeffrey T. Hancock,Tatsunori Hashimoto,Michael S. Bernstein
title: Jury Learning: Integrating Dissenting Voices into Machine Learning Models
title_zh: 陪审团学习：将异议声音整合到机器学习模型中
abstract: Whose labels should a machine learning (ML) algorithm learn to emulate? For ML tasks ranging from online comment toxicity to misinformation detection to medical diagnosis, different groups in society may have irreconcilable disagreements about ground truth labels. Supervised ML today resolves these label disagreements implicitly using majority vote, which overrides minority groups' labels. We introduce jury learning, a supervised ML approach that resolves these disagreements explicitly through the metaphor of a jury: defining which people or groups, in what proportion, determine the classifier's prediction. For example, a jury learning model for online toxicity might centrally feature women and Black jurors, who are commonly targets of online harassment. To enable jury learning, we contribute a deep learning architecture that models every annotator in a dataset, samples from annotators' models to populate the jury, then runs inference to classify. Our architecture enables juries that dynamically adapt their composition, explore counterfactuals, and visualize dissent.
summary: Whose labels should a machine learning (ML) algorithm learn to emulate? For ML tasks ranging from online comment toxicity to misinformation detection to medical diagnosis, different groups in society may have irreconcilable disagreements about ground truth labels. Supervised ML today resolves these label disagreements implicitly using majority vote, which overrides minority groups' labels. We introduce jury learning, a supervised ML approach that resolves these disagreements explicitly through the metaphor of a jury: defining which people or groups, in what proportion, determine the classifier's prediction. For example, a jury learning model for online toxicity might centrally feature women and Black jurors, who are commonly targets of online harassment. To enable jury learning, we contribute a deep learning architecture that models every annotator in a dataset, samples from annotators' models to populate the jury, then runs inference to classify. Our architecture enables juries that dynamically adapt their composition, explore counterfactuals, and visualize dissent.
abstract_zh: 机器学习 (ML) 算法应该学习模仿谁的标签？对于从在线评论毒性到错误信息检测到医学诊断等机器学习任务，社会中不同群体可能对真实标签存在不可调和的分歧。今天的监督机器学习隐式地使用多数投票来解决这些标签分歧，这会覆盖少数群体的标签。我们引入了陪审团学习，这是一种有监督的机器学习方法，它通过陪审团的比喻来明确解决这些分歧：定义哪些人或群体以何种比例决定分类器的预测。例如，在线毒性的陪审团学习模型可能以女性和黑人陪审员为中心，他们通常是在线骚扰的目标。为了实现陪审团学习，我们提供了一个深度学习架构，该架构对数据集中的每个注释器进行建模，从注释器模型中抽取样本来填充陪审团，然后运行推理进行分类。我们的架构使陪审团能够动态调整其组成、探索反事实并可视化异议。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为'陪审团学习'的新方法，通过整合不同群体的标注意见，替代传统多数投票机制，以更公平地处理机器学习中的标签分歧问题。
AI.motivation: 当前监督学习依赖多数投票解决标签分歧，但会压制少数群体意见，导致模型存在系统性偏差，尤其在涉及社会敏感议题（如网络毒性检测、医疗诊断）时问题突出。
AI.method: 设计深度学习架构建模每个标注者，通过抽样生成动态'陪审团'进行预测，支持群体权重调整、反事实探索和分歧可视化，实现标签分歧的显式处理。
AI.result: 实验验证了该架构能有效捕捉群体间分歧，动态调整陪审团组成后模型表现优于传统方法，在涉及边缘群体的场景中显著提升公平性。
AI.conclusion: 陪审团学习为AI决策提供了更透明的群体视角整合机制，通过显式建模分歧推动了算法公平性与可解释性的进步。

title: Improved Bounds for Fractional Online Matching Problems
title_zh: 改进分数在线匹配问题的界限
abstract: Online bipartite matching with one-sided arrival and its variants have been extensively studied since the seminal work of Karp, Vazirani, and Vazirani (STOC 1990). Motivated by real-life applications with dynamic market structures, e.g. ride-sharing, two generalizations of the classical one-sided arrival model are proposed to allow non-bipartite graphs and to allow all vertices to arrive online. Namely, online matching with general vertex arrival is introduced by Wang and Wong (ICALP 2015), and fully online matching is introduced by Huang et al. (JACM 2020). In this paper, we study the fractional versions of the two models. We improve three out of the four state-of-the-art upper and lower bounds of the two models. For fully online matching, we design a $0.6$-competitive algorithm and prove no algorithm can be $0.613$-competitive. For online matching with general vertex arrival, we prove no algorithm can be $0.584$-competitive. Moreover, we give an arguably more intuitive algorithm for the general vertex arrival model, compared to the algorithm of Wang and Wong, while attaining the same competitive ratio of $0.526$.
summary: Online bipartite matching with one-sided arrival and its variants have been extensively studied since the seminal work of Karp, Vazirani, and Vazirani (STOC 1990). Motivated by real-life applications with dynamic market structures, e.g. ride-sharing, two generalizations of the classical one-sided arrival model are proposed to allow non-bipartite graphs and to allow all vertices to arrive online. Namely, online matching with general vertex arrival is introduced by Wang and Wong (ICALP 2015), and fully online matching is introduced by Huang et al. (JACM 2020). In this paper, we study the fractional versions of the two models. We improve three out of the four state-of-the-art upper and lower bounds of the two models. For fully online matching, we design a $0.6$-competitive algorithm and prove no algorithm can be $0.613$-competitive. For online matching with general vertex arrival, we prove no algorithm can be $0.584$-competitive. Moreover, we give an arguably more intuitive algorithm for the general vertex arrival model, compared to the algorithm of Wang and Wong, while attaining the same competitive ratio of $0.526$.
abstract_zh: 自 Karp、Vazirani 和 Vazirani (STOC 1990) 的开创性工作以来，具有单侧到达的在线二分匹配及其变体已得到广泛研究。受到具有动态市场结构的现实应用的推动，例如在拼车方面，提出了经典单侧到达模型的两种推广，以允许非二分图并允许所有顶点在线到达。即，Wang和Wong（ICALP 2015）提出了一般顶点到达的在线匹配，Huang等人提出了完全在线匹配。 （JACM 2020）。在本文中，我们研究了这两个模型的分数版本。我们改进了两个模型的四个最先进的上限和下限中的三个。对于完全在线匹配，我们设计了一个 0.6 美元竞争的算法，并证明没有算法可以具有 0.613 美元竞争。对于一般顶点到达的在线匹配，我们证明没有算法可以具有 0.584 美元的竞争力。此外，与 Wang 和 Wong 的算法相比，我们为一般顶点到达模型提供了一种可以说更直观的算法，同时获得了 0.526 美元的相同竞争比。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了在线匹配问题的分数版本，改进了两种模型的上下界。
AI.motivation: 现实中的动态市场结构（如网约车）需要处理非二分图和所有顶点在线到达的情况，因此提出两种模型的改进。
AI.method: 设计新的算法并分析其竞争比，结合数学证明改进现有上下界。
AI.result: 为完全在线匹配设计了0.6-competitive算法，证明其下界为0.613；为一般顶点到达模型证明下界为0.584，并提供与原算法相同竞争力的更直观算法。
AI.conclusion: 改进了两种模型的三个上下界，提出了更易理解的算法，推动了在线匹配理论的发展。

authors_text: Seyyedali Hosseinalipour,Su Wang,Nicolo Michelusi,Vaneet Aggarwal,Christopher G. Brinton,David J. Love,Mung Chiang
title: Parallel Successive Learning for Dynamic Distributed Model Training over Heterogeneous Wireless Networks
title_zh: 异构无线网络上动态分布式模型训练的并行连续学习
abstract: Federated learning (FedL) has emerged as a popular technique for distributing model training over a set of wireless devices, via iterative local updates (at devices) and global aggregations (at the server). In this paper, we develop parallel successive learning (PSL), which expands the FedL architecture along three dimensions: (i) Network, allowing decentralized cooperation among the devices via device-to-device (D2D) communications. (ii) Heterogeneity, interpreted at three levels: (ii-a) Learning: PSL considers heterogeneous number of stochastic gradient descent iterations with different mini-batch sizes at the devices; (ii-b) Data: PSL presumes a dynamic environment with data arrival and departure, where the distributions of local datasets evolve over time, captured via a new metric for model/concept drift. (ii-c) Device: PSL considers devices with different computation and communication capabilities. (iii) Proximity, where devices have different distances to each other and the access point. PSL considers the realistic scenario where global aggregations are conducted with idle times in-between them for resource efficiency improvements, and incorporates data dispersion and model dispersion with local model condensation into FedL. Our analysis sheds light on the notion of cold vs. warmed up models, and model inertia in distributed machine learning. We then propose network-aware dynamic model tracking to optimize the model learning vs. resource efficiency tradeoff, which we show is an NP-hard signomial programming problem. We finally solve this problem through proposing a general optimization solver. Our numerical results reveal new findings on the interdependencies between the idle times in-between the global aggregations, model/concept drift, and D2D cooperation configuration.
summary: Federated learning (FedL) has emerged as a popular technique for distributing model training over a set of wireless devices, via iterative local updates (at devices) and global aggregations (at the server). In this paper, we develop parallel successive learning (PSL), which expands the FedL architecture along three dimensions: (i) Network, allowing decentralized cooperation among the devices via device-to-device (D2D) communications. (ii) Heterogeneity, interpreted at three levels: (ii-a) Learning: PSL considers heterogeneous number of stochastic gradient descent iterations with different mini-batch sizes at the devices; (ii-b) Data: PSL presumes a dynamic environment with data arrival and departure, where the distributions of local datasets evolve over time, captured via a new metric for model/concept drift. (ii-c) Device: PSL considers devices with different computation and communication capabilities. (iii) Proximity, where devices have different distances to each other and the access point. PSL considers the realistic scenario where global aggregations are conducted with idle times in-between them for resource efficiency improvements, and incorporates data dispersion and model dispersion with local model condensation into FedL. Our analysis sheds light on the notion of cold vs. warmed up models, and model inertia in distributed machine learning. We then propose network-aware dynamic model tracking to optimize the model learning vs. resource efficiency tradeoff, which we show is an NP-hard signomial programming problem. We finally solve this problem through proposing a general optimization solver. Our numerical results reveal new findings on the interdependencies between the idle times in-between the global aggregations, model/concept drift, and D2D cooperation configuration.
abstract_zh: 联邦学习 (FedL) 已成为一种流行技术，通过迭代本地更新（在设备上）和全局聚合（在服务器上）在一组无线设备上分发模型训练。在本文中，我们开发了并行连续学习（PSL），它沿着三个维度扩展了 FedL 架构：（i）网络，允许通过设备到设备（D2D）通信在设备之间进行去中心化合作。 (ii) 异质性，在三个层面上解释： (ii-a) 学习：PSL 考虑设备上不同小批量大小的随机梯度下降迭代的异质数量； (ii-b) 数据：PSL 假设一个具有数据到达和离开的动态环境，其中本地数据集的分布随时间变化，通过模型/概念漂移的新指标捕获。 (ii-c) 设备：PSL 考虑具有不同计算和通信能力的设备。 (iii) 邻近性，设备之间以及设备与接入点之间的距离不同。 PSL 考虑了现实场景，即在全局聚合之间有空闲时间以提高资源效率，并将数据分散和模型分散与本地模型压缩合并到 FedL 中。我们的分析揭示了冷模型与热模型的概念，以及分布式机器学习中的模型惯性。然后，我们提出网络感知动态模型跟踪来优化模型学习与资源效率权衡，我们证明这是一个 NP 困难的信号编程问题。我们最终通过提出通用优化求解器来解决这个问题。我们的数值结果揭示了关于全局聚合之间的空闲时间、模型/概念漂移和 D2D 合作配置之间的相互依赖性的新发现。

AI.tldr: 本文提出并行连续学习（PSL）方法，扩展联邦学习架构以解决异构无线网络中的动态分布式模型训练问题。
AI.motivation: 传统联邦学习在资源效率、数据动态变化和设备异构性方面存在挑战，需提升模型适应性和资源利用率。
AI.method: 通过设备间通信实现去中心化协作，考虑学习、数据和设备三层次异构性，引入数据/模型漂移度量，提出网络感知的动态模型跟踪优化方法，并设计通用求解器。
AI.result: 数值实验揭示了全局聚合空闲时间、模型漂移与D2D协作配置间的依赖关系，验证了PSL的有效性。
AI.conclusion: PSL通过动态模型跟踪和资源优化，在异构无线网络中实现了高效分布式训练，为未来边缘智能提供了理论支持。

search_text: Parallel Successive Learning for Dynamic Distributed Model Training over Heterogeneous Wireless Networks 异构无线网络上动态分布式模型训练的并行连续学习 Federated learning (FedL) has emerged as a popular technique for distributing model training over a set of wireless devices, via iterative local updates (at devices) and global aggregations (at the server). In this paper, we develop parallel successive learning (PSL), which expands the FedL architecture along three dimensions: (i) Network, allowing decentralized cooperation among the devices via device-to-device (D2D) communications. (ii) Heterogeneity, interpreted at three levels: (ii-a) Learning: PSL considers heterogeneous number of stochastic gradient descent iterations with different mini-batch sizes at the devices; (ii-b) Data: PSL presumes a dynamic environment with data arrival and departure, where the distributions of local datasets evolve over time, captured via a new metric for model/concept drift. (ii-c) Device: PSL considers devices with different computation and communication capabilities. (iii) Proximity, where devices have different distances to each other and the access point. PSL considers the realistic scenario where global aggregations are conducted with idle times in-between them for resource efficiency improvements, and incorporates data dispersion and model dispersion with local model condensation into FedL. Our analysis sheds light on the notion of cold vs. warmed up models, and model inertia in distributed machine learning. We then propose network-aware dynamic model tracking to optimize the model learning vs. resource efficiency tradeoff, which we show is an NP-hard signomial programming problem. We finally solve this problem through proposing a general optimization solver. Our numerical results reveal new findings on the interdependencies between the idle times in-between the global aggregations, model/concept drift, and D2D cooperation configuration. 联邦学习 (FedL) 已成为一种流行技术，通过迭代本地更新（在设备上）和全局聚合（在服务器上）在一组无线设备上分发模型训练。在本文中，我们开发了并行连续学习（PSL），它沿着三个维度扩展了 FedL 架构：（i）网络，允许通过设备到设备（D2D）通信在设备之间进行去中心化合作。 (ii) 异质性，在三个层面上解释： (ii-a) 学习：PSL 考虑设备上不同小批量大小的随机梯度下降迭代的异质数量； (ii-b) 数据：PSL 假设一个具有数据到达和离开的动态环境，其中本地数据集的分布随时间变化，通过模型/概念漂移的新指标捕获。 (ii-c) 设备：PSL 考虑具有不同计算和通信能力的设备。 (iii) 邻近性，设备之间以及设备与接入点之间的距离不同。 PSL 考虑了现实场景，即在全局聚合之间有空闲时间以提高资源效率，并将数据分散和模型分散与本地模型压缩合并到 FedL 中。我们的分析揭示了冷模型与热模型的概念，以及分布式机器学习中的模型惯性。然后，我们提出网络感知动态模型跟踪来优化模型学习与资源效率权衡，我们证明这是一个 NP 困难的信号编程问题。我们最终通过提出通用优化求解器来解决这个问题。我们的数值结果揭示了关于全局聚合之间的空闲时间、模型/概念漂移和 D2D 合作配置之间的相互依赖性的新发现。 本文提出并行连续学习（PSL）方法，扩展联邦学习架构以解决异构无线网络中的动态分布式模型训练问题。 Seyyedali Hosseinalipour,Su Wang,Nicolo Michelusi,Vaneet Aggarwal,Christopher G. authors_text: Qiang Zhang,Zeyuan Wang,Yuqiang Han,Haoran Yu,Xurui Jin,Huajun Chen
title: Prompt-Guided Injection of Conformation to Pre-trained Protein Model
title_zh: 快速引导构象注入预训练蛋白质模型
abstract: Pre-trained protein models (PTPMs) represent a protein with one fixed embedding and thus are not capable for diverse tasks. For example, protein structures can shift, namely protein folding, between several conformations in various biological processes. To enable PTPMs to produce task-aware representations, we propose to learn interpretable, pluggable and extensible protein prompts as a way of injecting task-related knowledge into PTPMs. In this regard, prior PTPM optimization with the masked language modeling task can be interpreted as learning a sequence prompt (Seq prompt) that enables PTPMs to capture the sequential dependency between amino acids. To incorporate conformational knowledge to PTPMs, we propose an interaction-conformation prompt (IC prompt) that is learned through back-propagation with the protein-protein interaction task. As an instantiation, we present a conformation-aware pre-trained protein model that learns both sequence and interaction-conformation prompts in a multi-task setting. We conduct comprehensive experiments on nine protein datasets. Results confirm our expectation that using the sequence prompt does not hurt PTPMs' performance on sequence-related tasks while incorporating the interaction-conformation prompt significantly improves PTPMs' performance on tasks where conformational knowledge counts. We also show the learned prompts can be combined and extended to deal with new complex tasks.
summary: Pre-trained protein models (PTPMs) represent a protein with one fixed embedding and thus are not capable for diverse tasks. For example, protein structures can shift, namely protein folding, between several conformations in various biological processes. To enable PTPMs to produce task-aware representations, we propose to learn interpretable, pluggable and extensible protein prompts as a way of injecting task-related knowledge into PTPMs. In this regard, prior PTPM optimization with the masked language modeling task can be interpreted as learning a sequence prompt (Seq prompt) that enables PTPMs to capture the sequential dependency between amino acids. To incorporate conformational knowledge to PTPMs, we propose an interaction-conformation prompt (IC prompt) that is learned through back-propagation with the protein-protein interaction task. As an instantiation, we present a conformation-aware pre-trained protein model that learns both sequence and interaction-conformation prompts in a multi-task setting. We conduct comprehensive experiments on nine protein datasets. Results confirm our expectation that using the sequence prompt does not hurt PTPMs' performance on sequence-related tasks while incorporating the interaction-conformation prompt significantly improves PTPMs' performance on tasks where conformational knowledge counts. We also show the learned prompts can be combined and extended to deal with new complex tasks.
abstract_zh: 预训练蛋白质模型 (PTPM) 代表一种具有固定嵌入的蛋白质，因此无法执行多种任务。例如，蛋白质结构可以在各种生物过程中的几种构象之间变化，即蛋白质折叠。为了使 PTPM 能够生成任务感知表示，我们建议学习可解释、可插入和可扩展的蛋白质提示，作为将任务相关知识注入 PTPM 的一种方式。在这方面，先前使用掩码语言建模任务进行的 PTPM 优化可以解释为学习序列提示（Seq 提示），使 PTPM 能够捕获氨基酸之间的顺序依赖性。为了将构象知识纳入 PTPM，我们提出了一种相互作用构象提示（IC 提示），它是通过蛋白质-蛋白质相互作用任务的反向传播来学习的。作为实例，我们提出了一个构象感知的预训练蛋白质模型，该模型可以在多任务设置中学习序列和相互作用构象提示。我们对九个蛋白质数据集进行了全面的实验。结果证实了我们的预期，即使用序列提示不会损害 PTPM 在序列相关任务上的表现，同时结合交互构象提示可显着提高 PTPM 在构象知识重要的任务上的表现。我们还展示了学习到的提示可以组合和扩展以处理新的复杂任务。

AI.tldr: 本文提出通过注入构象提示来增强预训练蛋白质模型的任务适应能力。
AI.motivation: 预训练蛋白质模型（PTPMs）使用固定嵌入表示蛋白质，无法处理需要构象变化（如蛋白质折叠）的多样化任务。
AI.method: 设计可插拔的序列提示（Seq prompt）和交互-构象提示（IC prompt），分别通过掩码语言建模任务和蛋白质相互作用任务进行学习，并在多任务设置中结合两者。
AI.result: 实验表明，序列提示不影响序列相关任务性能，而交互-构象提示显著提升了需要构象知识的任务表现，且提示可扩展至新任务。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过可解释的提示机制有效注入构象知识，提升了预训练蛋白质模型的多功能性与任务适应性。

title: Tractable Boolean and Arithmetic Circuits
title_zh: 易于处理的布尔和算术电路
comment: An earlier version of this article appeared in the following edited book. Pascal Hitzler and Md Kamruzzaman Sarker, editors. Neuro-Symbolic Artificial Intelligence: The State of the Art, volume 342 of Frontiers in Artificial Intelligence and Applications. IOS Press, 2021
comments: An earlier version of this article appeared in the following edited book. Pascal Hitzler and Md Kamruzzaman Sarker, editors. Neuro-Symbolic Artificial Intelligence: The State of the Art, volume 342 of Frontiers in Artificial Intelligence and Applications. IOS Press, 2021
abstract: Tractable Boolean and arithmetic circuits have been studied extensively in AI for over two decades now. These circuits were initially proposed as "compiled objects," meant to facilitate logical and probabilistic reasoning, as they permit various types of inference to be performed in linear-time and a feed-forward fashion like neural networks. In more recent years, the role of tractable circuits has significantly expanded as they became a computational and semantical backbone for some approaches that aim to integrate knowledge, reasoning and learning. In this article, we review the foundations of tractable circuits and some associated milestones, while focusing on their core properties and techniques that make them particularly useful for the broad aims of neuro-symbolic AI.
summary: Tractable Boolean and arithmetic circuits have been studied extensively in AI for over two decades now. These circuits were initially proposed as "compiled objects," meant to facilitate logical and probabilistic reasoning, as they permit various types of inference to be performed in linear-time and a feed-forward fashion like neural networks. In more recent years, the role of tractable circuits has significantly expanded as they became a computational and semantical backbone for some approaches that aim to integrate knowledge, reasoning and learning. In this article, we review the foundations of tractable circuits and some associated milestones, while focusing on their core properties and techniques that make them particularly useful for the broad aims of neuro-symbolic AI.
abstract_zh: 二十多年来，人工智能领域对易处理的布尔和算术电路进行了广泛的研究。这些电路最初被提议为“编译对象”，旨在促进逻辑和概率推理，因为它们允许以线性时间和前馈方式（如神经网络）执行各种类型的推理。近年来，易处理电路的作用显着扩大，因为它们成为一些旨在整合知识、推理和学习的方法的计算和语义支柱。在本文中，我们回顾了易处理电路的基础和一些相关的里程碑，同时重点关注它们的核心特性和技术，这些特性和技术使它们对神经符号人工智能的广泛目标特别有用。

AI.tldr: 回顾可处理布尔和算术电路的基础及其在神经符号AI中的应用
AI.motivation: 整合知识表示、推理与机器学习，推动神经符号AI发展
AI.method: 综述相关理论、核心属性及技术
AI.result: 总结电路特性与应用，促进跨领域方法整合
AI.conclusion: 可处理电路作为计算与语义基础，对神经符号AI具有重要意义

search_text: Tractable Boolean and Arithmetic Circuits 易于处理的布尔和算术电路 Tractable Boolean and arithmetic circuits have been studied extensively in AI for over two decades now. These circuits were initially proposed as "compiled objects," meant to facilitate logical and probabilistic reasoning, as they permit various types of inference to be performed in linear-time and a feed-forward fashion like neural networks. In more recent years, the role of tractable circuits has significantly expanded as they became a computational and semantical backbone for some approaches that aim to integrate knowledge, reasoning and learning. In this article, we review the foundations of tractable circuits and some associated milestones, while focusing on their core properties and techniques that make them particularly useful for the broad aims of neuro-symbolic AI. 二十多年来，人工智能领域对易处理的布尔和算术电路进行了广泛的研究。这些电路最初被提议为“编译对象”，旨在促进逻辑和概率推理，因为它们允许以线性时间和前馈方式（如神经网络）执行各种类型的推理。近年来，易处理电路的作用显着扩大，因为它们成为一些旨在整合知识、推理和学习的方法的计算和语义支柱。在本文中，我们回顾了易处理电路的基础和一些相关的里程碑，同时重点关注它们的核心特性和技术，这些特性和技术使它们对神经符号人工智能的广泛目标特别有用。 回顾可处理布尔和算术电路的基础及其在神经符号AI中的应用 Adnan Darwiche cs.AI,cs.CC,cs.LG,cs.LO
